title: SLA: Beyond Sparsity in Diffusion Transformers via Fine-Tunable Sparse-Linear Attention
core_contribution: SLA fuses sparse and linear attention into a single GPU kernel by exploiting the natural decomposition of attention weights into high-rank sparse and low-rank dense components, achieving 95% attention sparsity and 13.7x kernel speedup without quality loss.
summary: Attention computation is the dominant bottleneck in Diffusion Transformer (DiT) models for video generation. SLA addresses this by discovering that attention weights naturally decompose into a small high-rank component (<10%) and a large extremely low-rank component (>90%). It classifies attention weight blocks into critical (O(N²) FlashAttention), marginal (O(N) linear attention), and negligible (skipped), fusing all three paths into a single GPU kernel with both forward and backward passes. On Wan2.1-1.3B, SLA achieves 95% sparsity, 13.7x attention kernel speedup, and 2.2x end-to-end video generation speedup on RTX5090, while matching full attention quality with only 2000 fine-tuning steps.
key_findings: Attention weights in DiT decompose into high-rank sparse (<10%) and low-rank dense (>90%) components
SLA achieves 95% attention sparsity on Wan2.1-1.3B with no quality degradation
Fused GPU kernel delivers 13.7x forward attention speedup and 2.2x end-to-end speedup on RTX5090
Linear attention alone completely fails on video diffusion (VA: 0.042 vs 76.78)
Only 2000 fine-tuning steps needed — less than 0.1% of pretraining cost
Softmax outperforms ELU+1 and Hedgehog as the feature map in the linear attention component
limitations: Only evaluated on relatively small models (Wan2.1-1.3B, LightningDiT-1.0B)
Only tested on RTX5090; performance on other GPUs not reported
Requires fine-tuning — not a training-free drop-in replacement
Theoretical guarantees on when the sparse-lowrank decomposition holds are lacking

infra_impact.algorithm: Establishes the sparse-linear decomposition paradigm for efficient attention
infra_impact.kernel: Introduces novel fused kernel pattern (sparse + linear in one pass) with three optimization strategies
infra_impact.framework: Enables practical DiT video generation deployment by reducing attention cost
infra_impact.llm: The sparse-linear decomposition insight may apply to long-context LLM attention
infra_impact.agent: 2.2x end-to-end speedup could enable more responsive AI video generation in agents

open_questions: Does the high-rank/low-rank decomposition hold for autoregressive LLMs with causal masking?
Can SLA scale to 14B+ models and 100K+ sequence lengths?
How does SLA interact with quantization (FP8/INT4) for further efficiency gains?
